title: ABC Allocation — Two Activity Pools
event: Ortiz Co. uses ABC with two pools: setups ($120,000 budgeted, 300 setups) and machining ($240,000 budgeted, 12,000 machine hours). Product A requires 40 setups and 1,500 machine hours. Compute the overhead allocated to Product A.

computationPaths.narration: Each activity gets its own rate driven by its own cause — then products pay for exactly the activities they consume.

memorize.body: Per pool: rate = pool cost ÷ pool driver. Product's OH = Σ (rate × product's usage of each driver).
Low-volume products that demand many setups get hammered under ABC — that's the method revealing cost reality, not a math error.

questions.prompt: Using the pool rates above, total overhead allocated to Product A = ?

distractors.feedback: Machining only — Product A also consumed 40 setups at $400 each. Every pool allocates.
Setups only. Add the machining pool: 1,500 MH × $20 = 30,000 more.
